payload.description: Ignore daemon runtime regular files (.harness/daemon.pid + daemon.log) in the managed .gitignore block — close the #64 git add -A pothole

payload.reason: Two-actor plan review: Codex + independent Claude subagent BOTH REVISE with the identical under-fix (.harness/daemon.log is a second daemon regular-file runtime artifact, same git add -A hazard); v1 abandoned, v2 corrects scope to pid+log (sock excluded=unix socket). subagent re-verified fix against source → APPROVE. Everything else verified correct (render verbatim, TDD red fires, sync --gitignore command, no tier-2 anchor, no stale doc).

Two-actor code review both APPROVE, no BLOCKER/IMPORTANT (2 non-blocking MINORs: test_init.py subset out-of-scope by design; branch name cosmetic — renamed to match slug). Root-cause fix at injector; daemon.log under-fix caught in plan review.

checklist.note: Adds the two daemon runtime regular files (.harness/daemon.pid, .harness/daemon.log) to the injector's canonical path list; UDS socket correctly excluded (git never tracks a socket special file). Repo's own managed block re-synced to match _render_block().

Only the declared scope touched (injector + its test + .gitignore + plan). No tier-2 decision anchors gitignore_injector.py or .gitignore (verified). test_init.py partial subset left untouched by design (presence-only checks, already omits other lines) — documented in the plan.

Two-actor plan review caught the sole real issue (daemon.log under-fix, raised independently by Codex + Claude subagent) → v1 abandoned, v2 corrects scope; both actors then APPROVE. Two-actor code review both APPROVE, no BLOCKER/IMPORTANT. Comment accurately records the socket exclusion + the separately-handled .state.lock.

Reviewers audited all .harness/ runtime writes: the supervisor fallback log lands under .harness/operation-logs/ (already ignored); no other regular-file runtime artifact is left un-ignored. Socket cannot be swept by git add -A.
No doc surface enumerates the canonical gitignore list (verified: AGENTS.md, cli-reference, getting-started); nothing to regenerate. sync --check clean.

verdict.summary: Close the #64 dogfood pothole at the root: the daemon writes daemon.pid and daemon.log as regular files under .harness/, so git add -A during a lifecycle sweeps them into a commit (attest-verify then rejects the undeclared file). Add both to the injector's canonical path list so every init / sync --gitignore ignores them; socket stays excluded. 1644 tests pass; ruff / mypy / lint-imports (core-is-base KEPT) clean; decision check + sync --check clean. Two-actor plan review (converged after the daemon.log correction) + two-actor code review both APPROVE.
